MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz, via Zoom, for and with Laura Brockwehl, Trustee.

Mr. Schultz advises that Mr. Sandler has passed away. Mr. Schultz reports that the accounting has been delayed as his client has had issues obtaining insurance records and requests a continuance of 45 days to complete and file the accounting.

The matter is continued to Aug-02-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

The Court stated that this is the 2nd account, that there are a couple of issues, that there are investment advisor fees on top of full statutory fees, that whatever was appropriate to this point may not be now since the trustor is in his 90's. Ms. Alavi stated that the portfolio had significant gains and very small losses, that there was a gain of $42,000.00 after fees, that there was an overall income gain and that if the court does not feel the investments are appropriate they can be liquidated. The Court stated that it wants the trustee to think about it, that it is just a red flag, that it thinks it may not make good sense, that the trustee knows more about it and that the next report should address why it is appropriate to maintain or not. Ms. Alavi stated that in regards to the guideline fees, if the court is going to reduce the fees they would like time to amend the petition and to prepare time sheets and that otherwise her fees are less than what was paid to Merrill Lynch. The Court stated that it would make a difference if she can show that she spent a lot of time. Ms. Alavi stated that in regards to the allowance there was a clerical error, that check #238 was actually dated 1/29 and was the allowance for February, that the 2/29 check was the allowance for March and that he receives $500.00 each month which is monitored by Eden Villa for him. The Accounting petition was continued to August 23, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
